event_info: length outside off and hint of away movement; Marsh hangs back and plays it to the off side
oh outside edge and it has flown past the diving Padikkal at wide-ish first slip. On a length and moving away; Marsh plays away from the body and edges it. Gets off the mark with a streaky boundary
on a good length outside off; Marsh punched past cover to sweeper cover for a couple
full and angling in; Marsh walks across and pushes it to mid-wicket
full and outside off; Markram drives it to sweeper cover
there's protection in the deep for Bhuvneshwar Kumar. Overpitched and outside off; Marsh creams it to sweeper cover; just a single
he beats him first ball; back of a length and shaping away outside off; he's getting some good bounce here; Marsh is beaten all ends up
straying on the pads; Marsh works it away to deep square leg
right on the money; back of a length and around off stump; Markram taps it to gully
swinging into the pads; Marsh works it to mid-wicket; quiet over so far
slightly short and outside off; Markram punches it square of the wicket

he's found the outside edge again; in the channel just outside off; Marsh is squared up and the outside edge just falls short of short third man; Markram calls for a quick single and they make it comfortably in the end
back of a length and outside off; Marsh walks down the track and defends it to covers
on a length and angling in; no room for Marsh and he pushes it back to the bowler
angling into the pads; Marsh tucks it away to mid-wicket; this is a good start from the RCB bowlers
full blooded drive and Bhuvi has hurt himself as he bent low to stop that; full and on outside off; Markram drilled it down on the track and Bhuvi got some hand to it
back of a length and around off stump; no width; Marsh defends it to the off side

that's on the roof; monster hit from Marsh; knuckle ball from Bhuvi; short and Marsh had enough time in the world to hang back and launch it over deep mid-wicket; replacement ball is on the way. A 102m hit
back of a length and angling in; Markram is standing way outside the crease and pushes it to mid-off
in the channel just outside off; Marsh taps it to cover
straight back past long on; on a length and angling in; Markram just picks it and launches it through the line over long-on
angling in from outside off; Markram pushes it to mid-on
slower off cutter; Marsh didn't pick it and the ball goes off the inside half of the bat to mid-wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Devdutt Padikkal!! What a way to get out
event_info: it was a full-toss outside leg stump; Markram was looking to flick it through the leg side; but the bat face closed and he ended up getting a leading edge to cover; Dar strikes in his first over. Aiden Markram c Devdutt Padikkal b Rasikh Salam Dar 12(8) [6s-1]
this one hurried onto him; back of a length and angling in; Marsh was shaping in for the pull; but the ball goes off the inside edge to square leg
he goes for the wild slog and misses it completely; another pacy delivery just outside off; Pant was bit late into the shot
once again goes for the across the line shot and he's taken a blow on the body; banged in short and on the stump; Pant was beaten by the extra pace

short and outside off; Pant goes for the wild heave across the line and the ball goes off the inside edge towards the keeper; he's not able to connect anything here. In fact; replays show that it hit the left elbow
on an length and angling across the left hander; perfect test match length; Pooran defends it off the front foot
in the channel just outside off; teasing line; Pooran leaves it alone at the last minute; this is excellent from Hazlewood; giving nothing away
back of a length and slower outside off; Marsh is struggling to pick it as he punches it straight to the fielder at cover
full and tailing in; Marsh drives it down to long-off
full-toss on middle; Pooran flicks it straight to the fielder at mid wicket
banged in short on the stumps; Pooran ducks under it comfortably
they are not finding gaps today; slower and fuller; Pooran hits it straight to the fielder at covers
full and outside off; Pooran digs it to cover; lowest power play score for LSG in this season. 15 dot balls in the power play

finally he's been awarded as he was very accurate so far; back of a length and outside off; Pooran shapes into pull; but the extra pace outfoxed Pooran as he edges it back onto the stumps; RCB all over LSG at the moment. Nicholas Pooran b Josh Hazlewood 1(7)
he's off the mark; back of a length and in the channel outside off; first bad delivery from Hazlewood; Badoni stays back and guides it in the gap to third man; the deep fielder had no chance
1 run; straying on the pads; he's missed out on this one as the ball trickles off the pads towards square leg
tailing into the pads; Marsh works it to short midwicket
ouch that must have hurt; back of a length and this one hurried onto him as he was looking for the pull shot; missed it and is hit flush on the box
he's not able to connect anything today; another cross-seam delivery; Marsh was bit early into the shot and the ball just went over the off stump; fantastic stuff from Hazlewood
sliding on the pads; Badoni works it to deep square leg
slower through the air and on middle; Marsh pushes it to long-off
quicker and on the pads; Badoni flicks it off his legs to deep square leg
there you go; the bouncer banged in short on the stump; this one really got up and wide signalled
he's picked it; flatter and outside off; Marsh kneels down and slog sweeps it to backward square leg; that was muscled!
speared in full and outside off; way outside the wide line
he slowed it down a bit and pitches it on middle; Marsh was bit early into the shot and the ball goes off the inside edge towards the leg side
he launches this one over the bowler's head over long-on. Not a bad length; tossed up on middle; Marsh gets to the pitch of the ball nicely and hits it through the line into the stands
flatter and quicker; Badoni works it away to deep mid-wicket
he didn't read the wrong'un; the googly from back of the hand; Marsh didn't pick it and the ball races away off the inside edge to fine leg

quicker through the air and sliding onto the pads; Marsh gets down to play the sweep shot and misses it completely; it was only just clipping the leg stump; the original decision stays. Marsh then wandered down the pitch looking for a single and is sent back; the return is wide and that probably saved the batter
short and outside off; this one hurried onto him and he mistimes a pull to long-on
straying on the pads; Badoni works it away to deep square leg
the slider; full and on middle; Marsh flicks it to mid-wicket; that one kept a bit low
full toss and outside off; Marsh carves it to sweeper cover; just a single
tossed up and outside off; Badoni gets forward and defends it
he's been put away; the attempted bouncer; Badoni looked like he was waiting for that delivery; moves across to the offside and just hooks it away over fine leg
straying on the pads; Badoni works it to square leg

ball_event: out Bowled!! He's found the timber
event_info: the quicker one wide outside off; Marsh was hanging back in the crease and goes for the cut shot far away from the body and only manages to edge it back onto the stumps; the pace and the angle did him in. Mitchell Marsh b Krunal Pandya 40(32) [4s-3 6s-2]
full and sliding in; Samad works it to short mid wicket
he's found the gap; slightly shorter and outside off; Badoni rocks back and cuts it past the two fielders to deep backward point; he pierced the gap to perfection
flatter and on the pads; Badoni works it in the gap to deep mid-wicket and they come back for the second
slightly shorter and outside off; Badoni cuts it straight to the fielder at point
he's got it through; shorter and outside off; Badoni gets on top of the ball nicely and cuts it away to deep backward point
tossed up and outside off; Badoni hits it straight to the fielder at cover
flatter and shorter outside off; Badoni punches it down to long-on
flatter and shorter; punched off the back foot to sweeper cover

ball_event: out Caught by Rajat Patidar!! A soft dismissal
event_info: the slowness of the wicket foxed him; slower through the air and on the pads; Samad was looking to work it through the leg side; but got beaten by the slow pace and ends up getting a leading edge to cover. 100 wickets for Pandya in the IPL. Abdul Samad c Rajat Patidar b Krunal Pandya 0(2)
that's cheeky; full and outside leg stump; Choudhary gets across to the off side and laps it off his legs to fine leg; he gets off the mark in style
tailing into the pads; Choudhary flicks it to mid-wicket
flatter and outside off; Badoni works it nicely in the gap to mid-wicket; they run the first one hard and come back for the second
quicker and outside off; floated it up; Badoni can only manage to defend it to the off side
huge shout for a LBW but looks like it was going down; the wrong'un from back of the hand; Choudhary misses out on the sweep and misses it; it hit him high on the pads
quicker delivery on the middle; the slider; Choudhary goes back to defend and luckily gets an inside edge
another close call; Choudhary is not picking Sharma at the moment; another googly and Choudhary misses it completely as it hits him on the back leg
he's got one through; on the pads; Choudhary kneels down and paddles it past the keeper to fine leg
floated up; slower through the air; Choudhary gets forward and defends it; nicely bowled
much quicker and sliding into the pads; Choudhary works it away to deep square leg
tossed up on the leg-stump line; defended back at the bowler
hard hands from Mukul; but the ball drops well in front of the bowler - slower and at the stumps; had the right-hander pushing at that
tossed up on middle and leg; stabbed towards mid-on and Mukul wanted a single - he's a long way out when sent back; but the throw is slightly wide and he hurries back
flighted delivery on middle and leg; defended back at the bowler
thumped; tossed up on middle - Mukul leans forward; brings his wrists into play and then whips it over wide long-on for a maximum
pushed through by Krunal; worked into the gap wide of mid-wicket for a single
tossed up on middle; was the wrong 'un and it's punched down to long-on for a single
fraction short and at the stumps; worked off the back-foot to long-on for a couple of runs\
the wrong 'un; spins into the pads and it's clipped behid square on the on-side; they think about the second and decide against it
nicely played; tad too full from Suyash - Mukul gets down low; rolled his wrists on the sweep and places it behind square on the on-side; beats the man in the deep
tossed up just outside off - chopped hard and square on the off-side for a single
fraction short and wide of off - sliced away square on the off-side for a single
nicely played; the slower ball from Rasikh; but there's width on offer - Badoni waits on it and then runs it behind square on the off-side
another slower ball outside off - pushed into the gap wide of cover-point for a single
the bouncer from Rasikh; was the slower ball and Mukul drops his head away at the last moment; bounces through to the keeper
full-toss on the pads; whipped backward of square on the on-side for a single

ball_event: out Caught by Jitesh Sharma!! Edged and caught! The slower ball does the trick for Rasikh - was short and into the wicket
event_info: but there's no pace on offer and Badoni has to try and manufacture it on his own - went hard on the cut and gets a thick top-edge; safely through to Jitesh Sharma and the Super Giants lose their 5th wicket. Ayush Badoni c Jitesh Sharma b Rasikh Salam Dar 38(24) [4s-4 6s-1]
full-toss outside off; Pant throws his hands on the drive and missed
banged in short and into the pitch - Mukul shapes for the pull and mistimes it to mid-on

chance and Suyash has put it down; came to his left and at a good height; should've been taken at this level. The wide yorker and Pant was looking to run it down towards third man; opens the face of the bat and slices it to the left of short third
short and well wide of off - Mukul shuffles across and then swats it into the gap wide of long-on - DDP runs to his right and keeps them down to a couple
banged in short and just outside off - too high and past Mukul's swing; called as a wide for height
very full from Bhuvneshwar; jammed down to long-on for a single

ball_event: out Caught by Phil Salt!! That's a magnificent catch in the deep and it's Phil Salt -a low full-toss outside off and Pant brought his wrists into play on the whip over mid-wicket. Salt runs in from the deep
event_info: the ball was always dying onto him; but he opens up his body on the slide; full stretch and gets both hands under it; scoops it up and ensures that it didn't pop out when his hands hit the ground. Rishabh Pant c Phil Salt b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 1(6)
short of a good length and just outside off; pushed back at the bowler
fuller length delivery and slanting down leg; past Mukul's flick and is called as a wide
short of a good length and on off; a miscued pull past mid-wicket for a couple. Has to scamper; but he does so and completes the run
fuller length delivery outside off; driven down to long-off for a single
short of a good length and into the wicket - Linde mistimes the drive to cover
cheeky and four; banged in short and into the wicdket - Linde backs away; reverses the stance and gets enough on the scoop to clear the keeper
fuller length delivery at the stumps - Linde chips it back over the bowler's head and comes back for the second. David with the chase from mid-off
1 run; short of a good length and at the left-hander; Linde is late on the whip and the ball rolls off the thigh-pad to the off-side

a chance? Might've bounced in front of Shepherd - ran to his right at long-on and then dived; but the ball dropped in front of him - was full and Linde brought the bottom hand into play on the shovelled drive
yorker length delivery on the pads; whipped past square leg for a single

ball_event: out Caught by Tim David!! A second wicket for Bhuvneshwar
event_info: but this is another fine running catch; this time by Tim David. Short and into the wicket; the width is there and Linde throws his hands on the cut; hit high on the bat and the ball lobs over backward point. David runs a long way back and to his left; keeps his eyes on the ball and then completes a fine catch with his hands outstretched. He enjoyed it as well and he's chuffed to bits. George Linde c Tim David b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 7(7) [4s-1]

ball_event: out Bowled!! Cleaned up! A peach of a yorker and Shami has no chance - was full and tailed back in slightly - Shami was staying leg-side and can't get his bat down in time
event_info: the ball crashes into the middle and off-stump; knocking both the poles onto the ground. LSG are 8 down and Bhuvneshwar finds himself on a hat-trick. Mohammed Shami b Bhuvneshwar Kumar 0(1)
on a good length and well wide of off; dragged down to long-on for a single
short of a good length and well wide of off; slapped square on the off-side for a single
clubbed; Mukul can hit - picks the slolwer ball outside off; has to wait and then brings the bottom-hand into play as he shovelled it over long-on

ball_event: out Bowled!! Rasikh has his man and he celebrates with a smile - follows the slower ball with a very full ball - Mukul was looking to work it onto the on-side
event_info: opened up his left leg to try and flick it behind square; the ball crept through and the leg-stump is out of the ground. Mukul Choudhary b Rasikh Salam Dar 39(28) [4s-3 6s-2]
slower ball outside off - Digvesh is way too late on the poke and misses by a long way
slower ball outside off - toe-ended to the left of the bowler; Rasikh dives and makes the stop

1 run; another slower ball outside off; Digvesh swipes and misses - the ball rolls through to Jitesh Sharma and they decide to steal a bye. He has plenty of time on the under-arm shy and misses; with Avesh Khan short by a mile

ball_event: out Bowled!! Rasikh has four and LSG have been bowled out for 146. Keeps it full and keeps it straight
event_info: Avesh shuffles across on the lap; was looking to go over short fine and missed it completely - the ball crashes into the off-stump and the zing bails go flying once again. Avesh Khan b Rasikh Salam Dar 1(2)
on a good length and shaping away from the right-hander; Salt throws his hands on the drive and is beaten on the outside edge
short of a good length and at the stumps - Salt prods across and turns it to the on-side
cracking shot; back of a length and well wide of off - Salt gets on top of the bounce and then crunches it past cover for a boundary
short of a good length and swinging away from wide of off; Salt goes hard on the drive and is beaten on the top-edge
on a good length and just outside off; punched to the left of cover whee stand-in captain Pooran makes a diving stop

oh dear! A massive mix-up and Philip Salt would've been well short had the fielder picked the ball cleanly. Was a good length delivery outside off and Kohli dropped it in front of point; he set-off and then stuttered; so did Philip Salt; but Kohli then went for it. Philip Salt was stranded in the middle of nowhere; but the fielder took his eyes off the ball and let the chance go in waste
on a good length and just outside off - steered with an angled bat; behind square on the off-side for a single
short of a good length and at the stumps - Salt swivels across and muscles the pull; behind square on the on-side and he's back for the second
short of a good length and seaming away from the right-hander; drags Salt into the poke and beats him on the outside edge

ball_event: out Bowled!! There's the early wicket that LSG badly needed - short of a good length and around the off-stump channel
event_info: Salt shuffled across and was then looking to pull it over backward square leg; but the ball skids through and beats him for pace. The off-stump is knocked out once again and RCB have lost an early wicket. Phil Salt b Prince Yadav 7(8) [4s-1]
beautifully played; fuller length delivery on the pads - DDP lets the ball come and then flicks it off the pads; past square leg and races away to the ropes
short of a good length and just outside off - DDP went hard on the cut and mistimed it to the off-side
FOUR; too short and too straight from Shami; on the hips and Kohli helps it on its way with an inside edge on the flick; given as leg-byes; so must've come off his hips
short of a good length and at the stumps - Kohli brings his wrists into play and then whips it past square leg. Linde runs across to his left in the deep; slides and lets the ball go through
lovely; on a good length and well wide of off - Kohli stands tall and then punches it through the line; high and over cover for a boundary
on a length and at the stumps; tucked behind square on the on-side for a single
short and wide of off; flayed away behind square on the off-side for a single
full and at the pads; clipped behind square on the on-side for a single - 15 off the over
muscled; banged in short by Yadav - Kohli steps down the pitdch; swivels across and then pulls it over mid-wicket for a boundary
a swing and a miss - Yadav shortened the length and angled one back into the right-hander; Kohli backs away on the pull and missed
classy; back of a length and well wide of off - Kohli gets on top of the bounce and then steers it over cover-point; no need to run for those
on a good length and just outside off - Kohli presses across defensively
this is all too easy for Kohli - short and angling down leg; Kohli swivels across; gets inside the line and then pulls it behind square on the on-side for a boundary
short of a good length and angling down leg; past Kohli's flick and is called as a wide

a mistimed drive; but Kohli has got enough on the drive to clear mid-off - couple of bounces and over the ropes - stepped down the pitch and went through with the lofted drive; the bottom hand came off; but he has enough to clear the men in the ring
dragged short by Digvesh; pulled straight to square leg where the fielder fumbled and allowed a single
crunched; fraction short and just outside off - Kohli picks the length early; swivels across and then hammers the pull over deep mid-wicket for a maximum
pushed througfh by Digvesh; was the seam-up delivery and it's driven down to long-on
tad too short from Digvesh; whipped down to long-on for a single
flighted delivery outside off - Kohli goes back and pushes it to the bowler
flighted delivery on off; hi8nt of spin back into the right-hander and it's punched to the right of mid-off for a single
short of a good length and well wide of off; Kohli flat-bats the drive to the right of mid-off - Pooran gets across and then fumbles with the dive; allowing a second
short and just outside off - stayed a tad low; so did Kohli as he swivelled across on the pull; a single behind square on the on-side
short of a good length and angling into the left-hander; Padikkal hops across and defends it to the on-side
on a good length and justv outside off - steered with an angled bat past point. Brings up the 50-run stand
on a good length and around off; punched in front of cover for a quick single
the slower short ball outside off - Padikkal walks down the pitch and is early into the pull; misses and the ball bounces through to the keeper
92.7kph; short of length; Kohli goes on the back foot to pull it to deep midwicket
89.8kph; spinning away outside off; cut to deep point off the back foot
94.6kph; full on the stumps; Kohli drops it in front of cover and sets off. Gets sent back and luckily for Kohli; Pooran's throw is way off target
takes the single now as he pushes this full delivery to the left of cover
90.9kph; the bottom hand comes off the bat handle as Padikkal gets low to sweep hard. A single to deep backward square leg
93.2kph; flat ball wide of off; Kohli treks back to bunt it to long-on
129kph; short of length delivery angling in; Kohli moves leg-side to knock it to wide mid-on

round the wicket; short ball angling in. It's hip-high and Padikkal looks to go after it with a pull shot. Doesn't quite middle it and holes out to deep backward square leg. Devdutt Padikkal c (sub)Himmat Singh b Avesh Khan 10(11) [4s-1]

beaten! 132.8kph; length ball that may have nipped away a bit. Patidar has a tentative poke at it from the crease. The ball whistles past the outside edge and it wasn't too far away from the off-stump either
134.6kph; short of a good length; knocked towards mid-on
129.7kph; Kohli sees the width and looks to slash hard through the covers. Only manages to get an under-edge that hits the deck and dies down on the off-side
107.4kph; slower from Avesh and Kohli guides it to sweeper cover . Just 3 runs and a wicket off the over
well-directed bouncer from Shami! Bowled at 133.5kph; head-high and Kohli misses the pull. Shami and Kohli exchange smiles
134.1kph; straying onto the pads; Kohli tucks this short of a good length ball to fine leg
140.6kph; in-slanter on middle; Patidar stays in his crease to turn it to midwicket

that's a terrific hit from the skipper! Shami overpitches and Patidar swings hard with a big backlift to bludgeon it over long-on. Stand and deliver. Pace off and in the slot from Shami; duly dispatched
134.7kph; short and just over the left shoulder. Patidar spots the length early; swivels across and handsomely hooks it over fine leg with one leg off the ground. Back-to-back sixes for Patidar
fullish ball on off; push-driven back to the bowler
to long-off
88.5kph; fraction short and marginally outside off; cut hard to sweeper cover
87.2kph; more flight and fuller; Kohli drives along the ground to deep extra cover
88.1kph; Patidar mishits one. Shortish ball; Patidar backs away to try and go over extra cover. Miscues as he splices it to long-off
90.7kph; stays back and defensively pushes this length ball to point
Kohli rocks back and wristily whips it towards the non-striker and Patidar has little time to react. The skipper hops but it hits his legs. The batters can only take a single

ball_event: out Caught by Nicholas Pooran!! Second wicket for Avesh Khan. 133.1kph
event_info: fullish ball; Kohli tries to take the aerial route as he brings his bottom hand into play. Hits the inner part of the bat and hence the ball goes a lot straighter. Straight down long-on's throat. Kohli misses his fifty by one run. Replay shows that he may have checked his shot. Virat Kohli c Nicholas Pooran b Avesh Khan 49(34) [4s-6 6s-1]
134.6kph; back of a length; Jitesh Sharma pats it to point
132.8kph; short of length delivery; tapped into the off-side
131.2kph; back of a length outside off; Jitesh slashes uppishly for a single to sweeper cover
streaky four! Avesh has a wry smile. Extra bounce on a short of length; Patidar slashes and the ball flies fine of the short third man fielder off a thick outside edge
in-slanting back of a length delivery; Patidar knocks it through wide mid-on and keeps the strike
94.6kph; flat ball just outside off; Patidar eases it to long-on off the back foot
96.8kph; fraction short; punched to the right of deep cover off the back foot
that's an incredible shot from Jitesh Sharma. 94.8kph; fullish ball; Jitesh clears his front leg; bends his back leg and swings hard to hammer it inside-out over deep extra cover
94.6kph; Rathi pulls his length back. Jitesh Sharma sees that coming. Walks across; gets low and sweeps hard over square leg
92.7kph; full and just outside off; Jitesh Sharma presses forward and swings hard to loft it inside-out over wide long-off. The man at long-off is a mere spectator

Jitesh is toying with Rathi. 98.4kph; quicker one just outside off and that's enough for the wicketkeeper to put it away. Waits for it on the back foot to scythe it through point. 23 runs off the over
134.6kph; full on off; solid base; good swing of the bat and boom. Thumped over long-off. RCB are in a hurry

ball_event: out Caught by Digvesh Singh Rathi!! In the air and taken! The length isn't as full as the previous one. Rajat Patidar wants to loft another one downtown. Loses his shape in the process and hits it a long way up in the air. Rathi moves to his right from the mid-off region to take a safe catch. Patidar persishes in an attempt to finish the chase quickly. Rajat Patidar c Digvesh Singh Rathi b Prince Yadav 27(13) [4s-1 6s-3]
event_info: out Caught by Digvesh Singh Rathi!! In the air and taken! The length isn't as full as the previous one. Rajat Patidar wants to loft another one downtown. Loses his shape in the process and hits it a long way up in the air. Rathi moves to his right from the mid-off region to take a safe catch. Patidar persishes in an attempt to finish the chase quickly. Rajat Patidar c Digvesh Singh Rathi b Prince Yadav 27(13) [4s-1 6s-3]
fuller than a length; hardly any feet movement from Tim David who drives and inside-edges to mid-on

ball_event: out Caught by Mukul Choudhary!! There's an appeal for caught behind
event_info: up goes the finger and Jitesh is walking. Seam-up delivery; decent bounce from a good length. Jitesh shapes to hoick it across the line after shuffling across and gets an audible outside edge. Easy grab for the keeper. Jitesh Sharma c Mukul Choudhary b Prince Yadav 23(9) [4s-2 6s-2]
leg-lined short of length delivery; tucked to deep square leg
137kph; low full toss; push-driven to cover
googly with a slip in place; Shepherd stays back to push it through cover
full on drifting towards the pads; David pushes it to midwicket
90.7kph; full and drifting in towards leg; David flicks and misses. Something seems to have distracted David there
89.8kph; Rathi overpitches outside off; David leans across and slices the loft over wide long-off

that sums up LSG's day! Digvesh Rathi shortens his length. David rocks back to muscle it over midwicket and gets more height than distance as it comes off the bottom part of the bat. Samad runs to his left from deep midwicket and takes a good catch but while trying to flick it towards the converging fielder (Markram); he ends up lobbing it over the latter's head. Oh dear!
cut hard through point as the line is wide of off. Linde slides to his left in the deep but makes a mess of it. LSG have been sloppy
pushed towards cover
Shepherd pushes it to the right of backward point and there's a slight mix-up between the batters. Samad runs in and fails to collect the ball cleanly
steered to third man
very full outside off; driven hard to the left of the bowler. A single to long-off
pace off and on a length; Shepherd spots it and swings mightily to clobber it over long-on
pitched up ball; driven hard and along the ground to long-off
short of length just outside off; Shepherd waits on the back foot to scythe it wide of backward point. The ball bullets away to the fence and RCB win by 5 wickets
